title: Unique Crumbly Jamaican Papelon Skillet
description: A fresh and crumbly Jamaican dish featuring papelon, perfect for a low-sodium diet, this skillet is a show-off dish for any occasion. It combines the sweetness of papelon with the warmth of Jamaican spices.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add cold butter to the bowl and use a pastry blender to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in chopped scallions and jerk seasoning.
In a separate bowl, whisk together papelon and eggs until well combined.
Add the papelon mixture to the flour mixture and stir until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Pour the mixture into a 10-inch oven-safe skillet and smooth the top.
Bake the skillet in the preheated oven for 35 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is set.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
